With a team rating of 0.70, they are strong contenders for both playoffs and the championship.
3. Rajasthan Royals (RR): RR sits in third place with 8 points from 6 matches (4 wins, 2 losses), tied on points with RCB but behind on NRR (+0.631 vs +1.125). Despite their recent loss to KKR, they've maintained strong overall form. Their batting depth featuring Yashasvi Jaiswal and Sanju Samson combined with quality bowling makes them nearly unstoppable barring any major injuries. The Longshots: MI and KKR face an uphill battle. MI has only 2 points from 5 matches with a poor NRR of -1.104. While cricket remains unpredictable, their chances are slim unless there's a miraculous turnaround in form. CSK and LSG also face challenges with 4 points each from 6 matches.

⭐ Match Highlights: April 19, 2026

🔥 KKR vs RR (Match 28, April 19, Kolkata): Kolkata Knight Riders Won by 4 Wickets!

Venue: Eden Gardens, Kolkata

Toss: Not available

Match Summary: In a stunning upset, Kolkata Knight Riders finally secured their first win of IPL 2026, defeating Rajasthan Royals by 4 wickets with 2 balls to spare! This victory ended KKR's winless streak and gave them a glimmer of hope in their playoff race.

🏏 Key Performances (KKR vs RR):

  • Rinku Singh (KKR): Starred with the bat, scoring an unbeaten 53 runs off 34 balls, earning Player of the Match honors alongside Varun Chakravarthy. His composed innings anchored KKR's chase perfectly.
  • Anukul Roy (KKR): Provided crucial support with an unbeaten 29 runs off just 16 balls, hitting 2 fours and 2 sixes. His explosive finishing helped seal the victory.
  • Cameron Green (KKR): Contributed a quickfire 27 runs off 13 balls, including 4 fours and 1 six, accelerating the chase in the middle overs.
  • Rovman Powell (KKR): Added valuable 23 runs off 20 balls before falling to Ravindra Jadeja.
  • Varun Chakravarthy (KKR): Was the star with the ball, taking 3 wickets for just 14 runs in 4 overs. He dismissed Vaibhav Suryavanshi (46 off 28), Dhruv Jurel (5), and Riyan Parag (12 off 14). His variations were unplayable.
  • Sunil Narine (KKR): Made an immediate impact, taking 2 wickets for 28 runs in 4 overs, dismissing openers Yashasvi Jaiswal (39 off 29) and Donovan Ferreira (7 off 7).
  • Kartik Tyagi (RR): Led RR's bowling effort with 3 wickets for 22 runs in 1.4 overs, removing Angkrish Raghuvanshi (10 off 19), Ravindra Jadeja (9 off 7), and Ravi Bishnoi (0 off 2).
  • Vaibhav Suryavanshi (RR): Top-scored for Rajasthan with 46 runs off 28 balls, including 6 fours and 2 sixes, providing a solid platform.
  • Yashasvi Jaiswal (RR): Opened well with 39 runs off 29 balls, hitting 4 fours and 2 sixes before falling to Narine.

🔥 Match Turning Point

The KKR Bowling Masterclass: After RR posted 155/9 in their 20 overs thanks to Vaibhav Suryavanshi's 46 off 28 balls and Yashasvi Jaiswal's 39 off 29, KKR fought hard to chase down the target. Varun Chakravarthy's spell was absolutely brilliant, removing key batsmen at crucial junctures. Sunil Narine's early breakthroughs kept RR in check. In reply, KKR lost early wickets but Cameron Green's quickfire 27 off 13 balls set up the platform. Rinku Singh's unbeaten 53 off 34 balls and Anukul Roy's explosive 29 off 16 balls guided KKR home with 2 balls to spare, reaching 161/6 in 19.4 overs.

Impact on Standings: This victory moved KKR to 2 points from 7 matches (1 win, 5 losses, 1 no-result), improving their NRR to -0.631. It was KKR's first win of the season after starting with five consecutive defeats and one no-result, showing their resilience under Andre Russell's captaincy. Meanwhile, RR dropped to third place with 8 points from 6 matches (4 wins, 2 losses), reducing their NRR to +0.631. This loss shows that even the top teams are vulnerable in this tournament.

RR: 155/9 (20 overs) - Vaibhav Suryavanshi 46(28), Yashasvi Jaiswal 39(29), Varun Chakravarthy 3/14, Kartik Tyagi 3/22

KKR: 161/6 (19.4 overs) - Rinku Singh 53*(34), Anukul Roy 29*(16), Cameron Green 27(13), Sunil Narine 2/28, Varun Chakravarthy 3/14

The 19th edition of the Indian Premier League (IPL) is set to commence on March 28. Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru are set to play the opening game against Sunrisers Hyderabad at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru.
Last year, Bengaluru had a fantastic campaign, finishing second on the points table at the end of the league phase. They defeated Punjab Kings in the final to lift their maiden IPL title.
Apart from RCB, a lot of teams have assembled strong squads ahead of the new season. Many experts believe that this could be one of the most competitive IPL seasons in a long time.
The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) have announced the schedule for only the first 20 matches of the IPL. Announcing the entire schedule at once would have almost surely forced a few games to clash with the assembly elections in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, and Assam. The remaining schedule is expected to be announced at a later date.
IPL Points Table 2026 Rules
A victory will fetch two points, while a loss accounts for zero points accrued. In the event of a Draw, a point will be given to both the competing teams. Similarly, if a match is abandoned or cannot be concluded due to weather or external agents, both teams will secure one point each. In addition to points, the Net Run Rate will be taken in consideration for higher position on the table in case of equinumerous points among multiple teams. This metric becomes crucial as a tiebreaker in situations where two or more teams have the same number of points.

The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) have announced the schedule for only the first 20 matches of the IPL. Announcing the entire schedule at once would have almost surely forced a few games to clash with the assembly elections in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, and Assam. The remaining schedule is expected to be announced at a later date.
IPL Points Table 2026 Rules
A victory will fetch two points, while a loss accounts for zero points accrued. In the event of a Draw, a point will be given to both the competing teams. Similarly, if a match is abandoned or cannot be concluded due to weather or external agents, both teams will secure one point each. In addition to points, the Net Run Rate will be taken in consideration for higher position on the table in case of equinumerous points among multiple teams. This metric becomes crucial as a tiebreaker in situations where two or more teams have the same number of points.

They finished at 200/5 in 20 overs, falling short by 54 runs. PBKS bowlers, including Marco Jansen (2/37)and Vijaykumar Vyshak (1/30), maintained control in the middle and death overs, preventing any late surge from LSG’s middle order. The victory extended the Punjab Kings’ unbeaten run and strengthened their grip on the top spot of the IPL 2026 points table with an impressive net run rate. IPL 2026 Points Table impact These results brought major shifts in standings. Punjab Kings continued their dominance at No. 1 with 11 points from 5 wins and 1 no-result and a strong NRR of +1.420. On the other hand, Kolkata Knight Riders climbed off the bottom to the ninth position with their maiden win. They now have 3 points from 1 win and 1 no-result game and an NRR of -0.879. The Rajasthan Royals are in the third position below the Royal Challengers Bengaluru, with 8 points and an NRR of +0.599. KKR vs RR Highlights, IPL 2026: Rinku Singh’s fifty powers KKR to first win of the season; beat Rajasthan Royals by 4 wickets
KKR vs RR Live Score, IPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders finally opened their account in the season with a four-wicket win over Rajasthan Royals, riding on a composed unbeaten 53 from vice-captain Rinku Singh. RR had earlier been restricted to 155/9 after a strong start from Yashasvi Jaiswal (39) and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (46), who powered them to 81 without loss.
IPL 2026: Points Table | Schedule
The match turned in the middle overs as Varun Chakravarthy (3/14) and Sunil Narine (2/26) choked the scoring and triggered a collapse. Kartik Tyagi then struck three key blows at the death.
KKR, however, had a shaky chase, slipping early before Rinku Singh and Anukul Roy steadied the innings with a match-defining partnership to seal victory with two balls remaining.
KKR vs RR Playing XIs
Kolkata Knight Riders XI: Ajinkya Rahane(c), Tim Seifert(w), Cameron Green, Rovman Powell, Rinku Singh, Sunil Narine, Ramandeep Singh, Anukul Roy, Vaibhav Arora, Kartik Tyagi, Varun Chakaravarthy
Rajasthan Royals XI: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Yashasvi Jaiswal, Dhruv Jurel(w), Riyan Parag(c), Shimron Hetmyer, Donovan Ferreira, Ravindra Jadeja, Jofra Archer, Nandre Burger, Brijesh Sharma, Ravi Bishnoi
Commentary

20:04 (IST) Apr 19
IPL Live: KKR snap losing streak as Rinku Singh holds nerve in thriller
Kolkata Knight Riders finally ended their long wait for a victory in IPL 2026, edging past Rajasthan Royals by four wickets in a tense finish at Eden Gardens on Sunday.
A composed unbeaten 53 from vice-captain Rinku Singh guided KKR over the line with two balls to spare, as they finally broke a six-match winless streak after 22 frustrating days. The win also lifted them off the bottom of the table and offered much-needed relief to a side that had repeatedly faltered from winning positions earlier in the season.
In the end, KKR crossed the line with two balls remaining, completing a much-needed turnaround after repeated failures in tight finishes earlier in the season. The victory marked their first win in seven matches and offered a sense of stability to a campaign that had threatened to derail early.
For Rajasthan Royals, it was another game where a strong start with the bat was undone by a dramatic collapse and an inability to close out the chase despite early dominance. Their spin struggle in the middle overs and lack of death-overs penetration with the ball ultimately cost them the match.
READ FULL STORY
19:33 (IST) Apr 19
IPL Live Score: Rinku Singh leads the KKR chase with 53 not out
"Sometimes it happens like that when a few things don’t go your way, so you feel that intensity. But it wasn’t like I was hesitating or unsure about playing my shots. My mindset was to take the game till the end because I had conversations with the coach and also with my batting partner. The idea was to take it deep since the match was in a tricky situation. That’s what happens sometimes, sir. In that moment, the mind doesn’t work properly and you end up playing such shots. But thankfully, luck was on my side and we eventually won the match. Yes sir, that was in my mind as well. I had played quite a few innings and was waiting for a big one. This was a very good innings for me and also very important for my confidence. There’s nothing like a fixed position for me. I can bat at number five, lower down the order, or even at number four. Wherever the team needs me, I’m ready to bat in that position. This will give us a lot of confidence because this is our first win of the season. From here, we will carry this momentum forward." - Rinku Singh (53 not out off 34 balls)
19:27 (IST) Apr 19
RR vs KKR Live Score, IPL 2026: Royals skipper Riyan Parag after the loss
"I think for the first 4-5 overs, I thought it was a 170-run wicket, we fell 10 runs short. Errors from our side, we can't be dropping catches - that cost us the game. There was a lot of spin on the wicket, the way RJ and Punja bowled, I thought spin was the way to go. A few errors, but I thought spin was the way to go. He was almost ready to bowl the 14th and 16th over, but they lost wickets. Since they had two left-handers in, I bowled an over. The way the middle-order went about today, the platform was set. Myself and Hetty faced 15 balls each, but the execution went wrong. We thought 170 was a good score and we wanted to go hard in the last 5 overs, but that didn't happen. Everyone who plays for the Royals I have full confidence in them." - Riyan Parag

19:18 (IST) Apr 19
KKR vs RR Live Score, IPL 2026: It's all over! KKR beat RR by four wickets for their first win of the season
It has taken more than 20 days and seven matches for Kolkata Knight Riders to finally register their first win of the season. It was not a clinical performance by any means, but they will certainly be relieved to get off the mark.
Batting first, Rajasthan Royals were well served by a strong opening stand between Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yashasvi Jaiswal, who gave them a solid platform. However, the innings lost all momentum once the pair was separated, with wickets falling at regular intervals.
The middle overs belonged to Varun Chakravarthy and Sunil Narine, who bowled in tandem and picked up five wickets between them, choking the scoring and turning the game decisively.
At the death, Kartik Tyagi supported well with disciplined bowling to ensure RR could only manage 155 on the board, a total that ultimately proved below par.
In reply, Kolkata Knight Riders endured a nightmare start as both openers were dismissed without scoring, putting them immediately on the back foot. Cameron Green, fresh off a fifty in the previous outing, looked to counterattack with intent, but his brisk 13-ball 27 was cut short by a sharp stumping from Dhruv Jurel off Ravi Bishnoi’s bowling.
Despite the early blows, KKR managed to reach 51 in the powerplay, largely due to Green’s brief but aggressive cameo. However, Rajasthan Royals tightened the screws in the middle overs, with spin taking full control of the contest. Ravindra Jadeja was at his miserly best, choking the scoring rate and striking twice by removing Angkrish Raghuvanshi and Rovman Powell in quick succession, leaving KKR struggling to rebuild.
19:16 (IST) Apr 19
IPL Live: KKR need 9 off 6 balls
Jofra Archer goes for 12 crucial runs in the over as Kolkata Knight Riders seize control of the chase at the death.
Rinku Singh and Anukul Roy have tilted the equation firmly in KKR’s favour, shifting momentum at the perfect moment.
Now, KKR need just 9 runs from the final over to secure their first win of the season.

KKR vs RR Highlights, IPL 2026: Rinku Singh’s fifty powers KKR to first win of the season; beat Rajasthan Royals by 4 wickets
KKR vs RR Live Score, IPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders finally opened their account in the season with a four-wicket win over Rajasthan Royals, riding on a composed unbeaten 53 from vice-captain Rinku Singh. RR had earlier been restricted to 155/9 after a strong start from Yashasvi Jaiswal (39) and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (46), who powered them to 81 without loss.
IPL 2026: Points Table | Schedule
The match turned in the middle overs as Varun Chakravarthy (3/14) and Sunil Narine (2/26) choked the scoring and triggered a collapse. Kartik Tyagi then struck three key blows at the death.
KKR, however, had a shaky chase, slipping early before Rinku Singh and Anukul Roy steadied the innings with a match-defining partnership to seal victory with two balls remaining.
KKR vs RR Playing XIs
Kolkata Knight Riders XI: Ajinkya Rahane(c), Tim Seifert(w), Cameron Green, Rovman Powell, Rinku Singh, Sunil Narine, Ramandeep Singh, Anukul Roy, Vaibhav Arora, Kartik Tyagi, Varun Chakaravarthy
Rajasthan Royals XI: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Yashasvi Jaiswal, Dhruv Jurel(w), Riyan Parag(c), Shimron Hetmyer, Donovan Ferreira, Ravindra Jadeja, Jofra Archer, Nandre Burger, Brijesh Sharma, Ravi Bishnoi

20:04 (IST) Apr 19
IPL Live: KKR snap losing streak as Rinku Singh holds nerve in thriller
Kolkata Knight Riders finally ended their long wait for a victory in IPL 2026, edging past Rajasthan Royals by four wickets in a tense finish at Eden Gardens on Sunday.
A composed unbeaten 53 from vice-captain Rinku Singh guided KKR over the line with two balls to spare, as they finally broke a six-match winless streak after 22 frustrating days. The win also lifted them off the bottom of the table and offered much-needed relief to a side that had repeatedly faltered from winning positions earlier in the season.
In the end, KKR crossed the line with two balls remaining, completing a much-needed turnaround after repeated failures in tight finishes earlier in the season. The victory marked their first win in seven matches and offered a sense of stability to a campaign that had threatened to derail early.
For Rajasthan Royals, it was another game where a strong start with the bat was undone by a dramatic collapse and an inability to close out the chase despite early dominance. Their spin struggle in the middle overs and lack of death-overs penetration with the ball ultimately cost them the match.
READ FULL STORY
19:33 (IST) Apr 19
IPL Live Score: Rinku Singh leads the KKR chase with 53 not out
"Sometimes it happens like that when a few things don’t go your way, so you feel that intensity. But it wasn’t like I was hesitating or unsure about playing my shots. My mindset was to take the game till the end because I had conversations with the coach and also with my batting partner. The idea was to take it deep since the match was in a tricky situation. That’s what happens sometimes, sir. In that moment, the mind doesn’t work properly and you end up playing such shots. But thankfully, luck was on my side and we eventually won the match. Yes sir, that was in my mind as well. I had played quite a few innings and was waiting for a big one. This was a very good innings for me and also very important for my confidence. There’s nothing like a fixed position for me. I can bat at number five, lower down the order, or even at number four. Wherever the team needs me, I’m ready to bat in that position. This will give us a lot of confidence because this is our first win of the season. From here, we will carry this momentum forward." - Rinku Singh (53 not out off 34 balls)
19:27 (IST) Apr 19
RR vs KKR Live Score, IPL 2026: Royals skipper Riyan Parag after the loss
"I think for the first 4-5 overs, I thought it was a 170-run wicket, we fell 10 runs short. Errors from our side, we can't be dropping catches - that cost us the game. There was a lot of spin on the wicket, the way RJ and Punja bowled, I thought spin was the way to go. A few errors, but I thought spin was the way to go. He was almost ready to bowl the 14th and 16th over, but they lost wickets. Since they had two left-handers in, I bowled an over. The way the middle-order went about today, the platform was set. Myself and Hetty faced 15 balls each, but the execution went wrong. We thought 170 was a good score and we wanted to go hard in the last 5 overs, but that didn't happen. Everyone who plays for the Royals I have full confidence in them." - Riyan Parag

KKR vs RR Live Score, IPL 2026: It's all over! KKR beat RR by four wickets for their first win of the season
It has taken more than 20 days and seven matches for Kolkata Knight Riders to finally register their first win of the season. It was not a clinical performance by any means, but they will certainly be relieved to get off the mark.
Batting first, Rajasthan Royals were well served by a strong opening stand between Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yashasvi Jaiswal, who gave them a solid platform. However, the innings lost all momentum once the pair was separated, with wickets falling at regular intervals.
The middle overs belonged to Varun Chakravarthy and Sunil Narine, who bowled in tandem and picked up five wickets between them, choking the scoring and turning the game decisively.
At the death, Kartik Tyagi supported well with disciplined bowling to ensure RR could only manage 155 on the board, a total that ultimately proved below par.
In reply, Kolkata Knight Riders endured a nightmare start as both openers were dismissed without scoring, putting them immediately on the back foot. Cameron Green, fresh off a fifty in the previous outing, looked to counterattack with intent, but his brisk 13-ball 27 was cut short by a sharp stumping from Dhruv Jurel off Ravi Bishnoi’s bowling.
Despite the early blows, KKR managed to reach 51 in the powerplay, largely due to Green’s brief but aggressive cameo. However, Rajasthan Royals tightened the screws in the middle overs, with spin taking full control of the contest. Ravindra Jadeja was at his miserly best, choking the scoring rate and striking twice by removing Angkrish Raghuvanshi and Rovman Powell in quick succession, leaving KKR struggling to rebuild.

IPL Live: KKR need 9 off 6 balls

Rinku Singh and Anukul Roy have tilted the equation firmly in KKR’s favour, shifting momentum at the perfect moment.
Now, KKR need just 9 runs from the final over to secure their first win of the season.

Still, just seven runs come off the over, ensuring the pressure stays firmly on Kolkata Knight Riders. The chase is finely poised now, with KKR needing 21 runs from the final two overs.

It’s a complete turnaround as KKR collect 19 crucial runs, bringing the required rate down to around 10 an over. The momentum is finally swinging their way at the right time in the chase.

Touch quicker, full and just outside off stump, Ramandeep leans across looking to drive. He reaches for it away from his body, but only manages an inside edge that drags straight back onto the stumps.
Punja is delighted as the bails go flying — his first IPL wicket — and Ramandeep stands in disbelief for a moment before sinking to his haunches, clearly frustrated, before making the long walk back.

Powell holes out as Jadeja strikes for the second time. Bowled at 92.8 kph, stump-to-stump on a good length, forcing the error. Powell advances down the track and clears his front leg looking to launch it over long-on, but he is nowhere near the pitch of the ball.
The shot comes off the lower part of the bat and lacks timing, ballooning safely into the hands of Donovan Ferreira. A crucial breakthrough in the middle overs for the Royals.

BIG WICKET! Ravindra Jadeja comes into the attack and removes in-form Angkrish Raghuvanshi (10). KKR also lost a review
Jadeja delivers a superb over, conceding just one run and striking a crucial blow as Raghuvanshi falls LBW.
Raghuvanshi attempts the reverse sweep, but it’s a risky option against Jadeja’s precision and control. The full delivery skids in and beats him for pace, striking him right in front of off stump.

IPL Live: OUT! Bishnoi strikes to dismiss Green
STUMPED! What an effort from Druv Jurel behind the stumps! Cameron Green is walking. Ravi Bishnoi is ecstatic.
What a piece of work from Dhruv Jurel! Bishnoi sees Green charge down the track and drifts in a googly down the leg side. The ball turns further away, making life difficult for the keeper as he’s momentarily blinded by the batter’s movement.
But Jurel reacts brilliantly — diving to his left, gathering cleanly one-handed, and firing a direct hit at the stumps in one fluid motion. Green is well out of his crease and cannot make it back in time. The third umpire is called in, but the decision stands as correct.

IPL Live: Royals collapse from 81/0 to set KKR a 156-run target
The spin duo of Varun Chakravarthy and Sunil Narine delivered a clinical performance to restrict Rajasthan Royals to 155/9 against Kolkata Knight Riders in their IPL clash.
Asked to bat, RR got off to a blazing start through Yashasvi Jaiswal (39) and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (46), racing to 81/0 in just 8.3 overs. However, the introduction of spin turned the game on its head. Chakravarthy (3/14) and Narine (2/26) applied the brakes effectively, triggering a collapse that saw RR slip to 124/5 by the 16th over.
Despite brief resistance from Shimron Hetmyer (15) and Riyan Parag (12), the innings never regained momentum. Kartik Tyagi (3/22) then struck thrice in a crucial phase to derail the lower order, ensuring RR were kept to a below-par total.

(On Varun Chakaravarthy) We talk a lot about cricket. Once he's back in form, it's good for our team. It's all about the first over, he bowled the first over good and that gives the momentum. Powerplay is the best time to bat." - Sunil Narine

Sensing an opportunity, the batters go for a risky second, but Arora recovers quickly, gathers cleanly and fires a sharp throw back to the striker’s end. Seifert is alert this time, whipping off all three stumps in a flash—with Archer nowhere in the frame.

After a blazing start, Rajasthan Royals have completely lost their way at Eden Gardens.

Key Insights:

1. Punjab Kings (PBKS): The team continues to lead the table with an impressive 11 points from 6 matches (5 wins, 0 losses, 1 no-result), maintaining their unbeaten run throughout the tournament. Their NRR of +1.420 is exceptional and reflects their dominant performances. Under Shreyas Iyer's leadership, PBKS has shown remarkable consistency with their balanced squad featuring Priyansh Arya, Cooper Connolly, and a strong bowling attack led by Marco Jansen and Vijaykumar Vyshak. With a team rating of 0.72, they remain favorites for both playoffs and potentially the championship.
2. Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB): After their latest results, RCB sits in second place with 8 points from 6 matches (4 wins, 2 losses), boasting an excellent NRR of +1.171. Virat Kohli continues to be instrumental, while their bowling attack led by Bhuvneshwar Kumar and Josh Hazlewood has been clinical. However, their recent loss to DC shows vulnerability. With a team rating of 0.70, they are strong contenders for both playoffs and the championship.
3. Rajasthan Royals (RR): RR sits in third place with 8 points from 6 matches (4 wins, 2 losses), tied on points with RCB but behind on NRR (+0.599 vs +1.171). Despite their recent loss to KKR, they've maintained strong overall form. Their batting depth featuring Yashasvi Jaiswal and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi combined with quality bowling makes them nearly unstoppable barring any major injuries. With a team rating of 0.65, they are strong contenders for a top-two playoff position.
4. Battle for Fourth Spot: This is where the real drama unfolds! SRH, DC, and GT are locked in an intense battle for the remaining playoff positions. All three teams sit on 6 points each, creating a tight race. SRH leads this pack with the best NRR (+0.566), followed by DC (+0.310) and GT (+0.018). Head-to-head records and net run rate will be critical tiebreakers. Every remaining match will be a must-win for these teams.
The Longshots: MI and KKR face an uphill battle. MI has only 2 points from 5 matches with a poor NRR of -1.076. KKR's situation improved dramatically with their first-ever win of the season against RR on April 19, but they still have just 3 points from 7 matches (1 win, 5 losses, 1 no-result) and an NRR of -0.879. While cricket remains unpredictable, their chances are slim unless there's a miraculous turnaround in form. CSK and LSG also face challenges with 4 points each from 6 matches.

⭐ Match Highlights: April 19, 2026

🔥 KKR vs RR (Match 28, April 19, Kolkata): Kolkata Knight Riders Won by 4 Wickets!

Venue: Eden Gardens, Kolkata

Toss: RR won the toss and elected to bat first

Match Summary: In a stunning upset, Kolkata Knight Riders finally secured their first win of IPL 2026, defeating Rajasthan Royals by 4 wickets with 2 balls to spare! This victory ended KKR's winless streak of 6 matches (including 1 no-result) and gave them a glimmer of hope in their playoff race.

🏏 Key Performances (KKR vs RR):

  • Rinku Singh (KKR): Starred with the bat, scoring an unbeaten 53 runs off 34 balls, earning Player of the Match honors alongside Varun Chakravarthy. His composed innings anchored KKR's chase perfectly.
  • Anukul Roy (KKR): Provided crucial support with an unbeaten 29 runs off just 16 balls, hitting 2 fours and 2 sixes. His explosive finishing helped seal the victory.
  • Cameron Green (KKR): Contributed a quickfire 27 runs off 13 balls, including 4 fours and 1 six, accelerating the chase in the middle overs before being stumped brilliantly by Dhruv Jurel.
  • Rovman Powell (KKR): Added valuable 23 runs off 20 balls before falling to Ravindra Jadeja.
  • Varun Chakravarthy (KKR): Was the star with the ball, taking 3 wickets for just 14 runs in 4 overs. He dismissed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (46 off 28), Dhruv Jurel (5), and Riyan Parag (12 off 14). His variations were unplayable. Earned Player of the Match award.
  • Sunil Narine (KKR): Made an immediate impact, taking 2 wickets for 26 runs in 4 overs, dismissing openers Yashasvi Jaiswal (39 off 29) and Donovan Ferreira (7 off 7).
  • Kartik Tyagi (KKR): Led KKR's death bowling effort with 3 wickets for 22 runs in 3.4 overs, removing Ravindra Jadeja (9 off 7), Shimron Hetmyer (15), and Ravi Bishnoi (0 off 2).
  • Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (RR): Top-scored for Rajasthan with 46 runs off 28 balls, including 6 fours and 2 sixes, providing a solid platform before falling to Chakravarthy.
  • Yashasvi Jaiswal (RR): Opened well with 39 runs off 29 balls, hitting 4 fours and 2 sixes before falling to Narine.
  • Dhruv Jurel (RR): Showcased brilliant keeping skills with a spectacular one-handed stumping of Cameron Green, which went viral worldwide.

🔥 PBKS vs LSG (Match 29, April 19, New Chandigarh): Punjab Kings Won by 54 Runs!

Venue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, New Chandigarh

Match Summary: Punjab Kings produced a high-voltage batting display, posting the highest total of IPL 2026 so far - a massive 254/7 in 20 overs. They crushed Lucknow Super Giants by a commanding 54 runs, extending their unbeaten run and strengthening their grip on the top spot.

🏏 Key Performances (PBKS vs LSG):

  • Priyansh Arya (PBKS): Young sensation smashed an explosive 93 runs off just 37 balls, leading the charge with aggressive strokeplay and walking away with Player of the Match honours.
  • Cooper Connolly (PBKS): Anchored the innings brilliantly with a magnificent 87 runs off 46 balls, forming a crucial partnership with Priyansh Arya.
  • Marco Jansen (PBKS): Maintained control in the middle and death overs with figures of 2/37, preventing any late surge from LSG's middle order.
  • Vijaykumar Vyshak (PBKS): Contributed 1/30 in his 4 overs, helping keep LSG in check during the chase.

🔥 Match Turning Point (KKR vs RR)

The KKR Bowling Masterclass: After RR posted 155/9 in their 20 overs thanks to Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's 46 off 28 balls and Yashasvi Jaiswal's 39 off 29, KKR fought hard to chase down the target. Varun Chakravarthy's spell was absolutely brilliant, removing key batsmen at crucial junctures. Sunil Narine's early breakthroughs kept RR in check. In reply, KKR lost early wickets as both openers fell for ducks, but Cameron Green's quickfire 27 off 13 balls set up the platform. Rinku Singh's unbeaten 53 off 34 balls and Anukul Roy's explosive 29 off 16 balls guided KKR home with 2 balls to spare, reaching 161/6 in 19.4 overs.

Impact on Standings: The KKR victory moved them to 3 points from 7 matches (1 win, 5 losses, 1 no-result), improving their NRR to -0.879. It was KKR's first win of the season after starting with five consecutive defeats and one no-result, showing their resilience under Ajinkya Rahane's captaincy. Meanwhile, RR dropped to third place with 8 points from 6 matches (4 wins, 2 losses), reducing their NRR to +0.599. PBKS extended their lead at the top with 11 points and an NRR of +1.420 after their dominant 54-run victory over LSG.

The 19th edition of the Indian Premier League (IPL) is set to commence on March 28. Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru are set to play the opening game against Sunrisers Hyderabad at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru.
Last year, Bengaluru had a fantastic campaign, finishing second on the points table at the end of the league phase. They defeated Punjab Kings in the final to lift their maiden IPL title.
Apart from RCB, a lot of teams have assembled strong squads ahead of the new season. Many experts believe that this could be one of the most competitive IPL seasons in a long time.
The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) have announced the schedule for only the first 20 matches of the IPL. Announcing the entire schedule at once would have almost surely forced a few games to clash with the assembly elections in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, and Assam. The remaining schedule is expected to be announced at a later date.
IPL Points Table 2026 Rules
A victory will fetch two points, while a loss accounts for zero points accrued. In the event of a Draw, a point will be given to both the competing teams. Similarly, if a match is abandoned or cannot be concluded due to weather or external agents, both teams will secure one point each. In addition to points, the Net Run Rate will be taken in consideration for higher position on the table in case of equinumerous points among multiple teams. This metric becomes crucial as a tiebreaker in situations where two or more teams have the same number of points.

Aachal ManiyarPublished20 Apr 2026, 05:00 AM IST Punjab Kings' Priyansh Arya and Cooper Connolly celebrate their partnership during the Indian Premier League 2026 match against Lucknow Super Giants at Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, in New Chandigarh, Mullanpur.(ANI Pic Service) AI Quick Read Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) finally tasted victory in the IPL 2026 season after a long wait, defeating Rajasthan Royals (RR) by four wickets in a dramatic last-over finish at Eden Gardens on Sunday (April 19). In the second match of the Sunday double header, Punjab Kings (PBKS) produced a high-voltage batting display to crush Lucknow Super Giants by a commanding 54 runs at New Chandigarh. KKR vs RR: Rinku Singh and spinners script KKR’s first win of the season RR won the toss and batted first on a slow Eden Gardens pitch but could only manage 155/9 in 20 overs. KKR’s spinners, led by Varun Chakravarthy (3/14), applied relentless pressure, breaking partnerships and restricting the Royals’ big hitters. Chakravarthy’s economical and wicket-taking spell earned him the well-deserved Player of the Match award. Chasing 156, KKR stumbled early but found a hero in Rinku Singh, who smashed a crucial 53 off 34 balls. Young Anukul Roy chipped in with a quick 29 off 16 deliveries, building a vital lower-order partnership. With two balls remaining and the equation tight, Rinku sealed the deal with a massive six over fine leg. This win ended KKR’s six-match winless streak (including one no-result) and injected fresh hope into their campaign. Rajasthan Royals, despite the narrow loss, remained in the top half of the table thanks to their strong start to the season. Also Read | Dhruv Jurel's acrobatic stumping in KKR vs RR grabs worldwide attention PBKS vs LSG: Punjab Kings unleash season’s highest total to dominate Lucknow Super Giants In the evening clash at Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Punjab Kings electrified the crowd with an explosive batting performance. PBKS posted a massive 254/7 in 20 overs, the highest total of IPL 2026 so far. Young sensation Priyansh Arya 93 (37) led the charge with aggressive strokeplay and walked away with Player of the Match honours. He was well supported by Cooper Connolly 87 (46), who anchored the innings from the other end. Also Read | Aiden Markram bowls 32-run over; sets unwanted IPL record Set a daunting target of 255, Lucknow Super Giants never found momentum. They finished at 200/5 in 20 overs, falling short by 54 runs. PBKS bowlers, including Marco Jansen (2/37)and Vijaykumar Vyshak (1/30), maintained control in the middle and death overs, preventing any late surge from LSG’s middle order. The victory extended the Punjab Kings’ unbeaten run and strengthened their grip on the top spot of the IPL 2026 points table with an impressive net run rate. IPL 2026 Points Table impact These results brought major shifts in standings. Punjab Kings continued their dominance at No. 1 with 11 points from 5 wins and 1 no-result and a strong NRR of +1.420. On the other hand, Kolkata Knight Riders climbed off the bottom to the ninth position with their maiden win. They now have 3 points from 1 win and 1 no-result game and an NRR of -0.879. The Rajasthan Royals are in the third position below the Royal Challengers Bengaluru, with 8 points and an NRR of +0.599. Lucknow Super Giants remained in the lower half (8th position), struggling to chase big totals. They have 4 points and an NRR of -1.173. KKR vs RR Highlights, IPL 2026: Rinku Singh’s fifty powers KKR to first win of the season; beat Rajasthan Royals by 4 wickets
THE TIMES OF INDIA | Apr 19, 2026, 20:04:17 IST
Share
KKR vs RR Highlights, IPL 2026: Rinku Singh’s fifty powers KKR to first win of the season; beat Rajasthan Royals by 4 wickets
KKR vs RR Live Score, IPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders finally opened their account in the season with a four-wicket win over Rajasthan Royals, riding on a composed unbeaten 53 from vice-captain Rinku Singh. RR had earlier been restricted to 155/9 after a strong start from Yashasvi Jaiswal (39) and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (46), who powered them to 81 without loss.
IPL 2026: Points Table | Schedule
The match turned in the middle overs as Varun Chakravarthy (3/14) and Sunil Narine (2/26) choked the scoring and triggered a collapse. Kartik Tyagi then struck three key blows at the death.
KKR, however, had a shaky chase, slipping early before Rinku Singh and Anukul Roy steadied the innings with a match-defining partnership to seal victory with two balls remaining.
KKR vs RR Playing XIs
Kolkata Knight Riders XI: Ajinkya Rahane(c), Tim Seifert(w), Cameron Green, Rovman Powell, Rinku Singh, Sunil Narine, Ramandeep Singh, Anukul Roy, Vaibhav Arora, Kartik Tyagi, Varun Chakaravarthy
Rajasthan Royals XI: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Yashasvi Jaiswal, Dhruv Jurel(w), Riyan Parag(c), Shimron Hetmyer, Donovan Ferreira, Ravindra Jadeja, Jofra Archer, Nandre Burger, Brijesh Sharma, Ravi Bishnoi

20:04 (IST) Apr 19
IPL Live: KKR snap losing streak as Rinku Singh holds nerve in thriller
Kolkata Knight Riders finally ended their long wait for a victory in IPL 2026, edging past Rajasthan Royals by four wickets in a tense finish at Eden Gardens on Sunday.
A composed unbeaten 53 from vice-captain Rinku Singh guided KKR over the line with two balls to spare, as they finally broke a six-match winless streak after 22 frustrating days. The win also lifted them off the bottom of the table and offered much-needed relief to a side that had repeatedly faltered from winning positions earlier in the season.
In the end, KKR crossed the line with two balls remaining, completing a much-needed turnaround after repeated failures in tight finishes earlier in the season. The victory marked their first win in seven matches and offered a sense of stability to a campaign that had threatened to derail early.
For Rajasthan Royals, it was another game where a strong start with the bat was undone by a dramatic collapse and an inability to close out the chase despite early dominance. Their spin struggle in the middle overs and lack of death-overs penetration with the ball ultimately cost them the match.
READ FULL STORY
19:33 (IST) Apr 19
IPL Live Score: Rinku Singh leads the KKR chase with 53 not out
"Sometimes it happens like that when a few things don’t go your way, so you feel that intensity. But it wasn’t like I was hesitating or unsure about playing my shots. My mindset was to take the game till the end because I had conversations with the coach and also with my batting partner. The idea was to take it deep since the match was in a tricky situation. That’s what happens sometimes, sir. In that moment, the mind doesn’t work properly and you end up playing such shots. But thankfully, luck was on my side and we eventually won the match. Yes sir, that was in my mind as well. I had played quite a few innings and was waiting for a big one. This was a very good innings for me and also very important for my confidence. There’s nothing like a fixed position for me. I can bat at number five, lower down the order, or even at number four. Wherever the team needs me, I’m ready to bat in that position. This will give us a lot of confidence because this is our first win of the season. From here, we will carry this momentum forward." - Rinku Singh (53 not out off 34 balls)
19:27 (IST) Apr 19
RR vs KKR Live Score, IPL 2026: Royals skipper Riyan Parag after the loss
"I think for the first 4-5 overs, I thought it was a 170-run wicket, we fell 10 runs short. Errors from our side, we can't be dropping catches - that cost us the game. There was a lot of spin on the wicket, the way RJ and Punja bowled, I thought spin was the way to go. A few errors, but I thought spin was the way to go. He was almost ready to bowl the 14th and 16th over, but they lost wickets. Since they had two left-handers in, I bowled an over. The way the middle-order went about today, the platform was set. Myself and Hetty faced 15 balls each, but the execution went wrong. We thought 170 was a good score and we wanted to go hard in the last 5 overs, but that didn't happen. Everyone who plays for the Royals I have full confidence in them." - Riyan Parag

KKR vs RR Live Score, IPL 2026: It's all over! KKR beat RR by four wickets for their first win of the season
It has taken more than 20 days and seven matches for Kolkata Knight Riders to finally register their first win of the season. It was not a clinical performance by any means, but they will certainly be relieved to get off the mark.
Batting first, Rajasthan Royals were well served by a strong opening stand between Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yashasvi Jaiswal, who gave them a solid platform. However, the innings lost all momentum once the pair was separated, with wickets falling at regular intervals.
The middle overs belonged to Varun Chakravarthy and Sunil Narine, who bowled in tandem and picked up five wickets between them, choking the scoring and turning the game decisively.
At the death, Kartik Tyagi supported well with disciplined bowling to ensure RR could only manage 155 on the board, a total that ultimately proved below par.
In reply, Kolkata Knight Riders endured a nightmare start as both openers were dismissed without scoring, putting them immediately on the back foot. Cameron Green, fresh off a fifty in the previous outing, looked to counterattack with intent, but his brisk 13-ball 27 was cut short by a sharp stumping from Dhruv Jurel off Ravi Bishnoi’s bowling.
Despite the early blows, KKR managed to reach 51 in the powerplay, largely due to Green’s brief but aggressive cameo. However, Rajasthan Royals tightened the screws in the middle overs, with spin taking full control of the contest. Ravindra Jadeja was at his miserly best, choking the scoring rate and striking twice by removing Angkrish Raghuvanshi and Rovman Powell in quick succession, leaving KKR struggling to rebuild.

IPL Live: Royals collapse from 81/0 to set KKR a 156-run target
The spin duo of Varun Chakravarthy and Sunil Narine delivered a clinical performance to restrict Rajasthan Royals to 155/9 against Kolkata Knight Riders in their IPL clash.
Asked to bat, RR got off to a blazing start through Yashasvi Jaiswal (39) and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (46), racing to 81/0 in just 8.3 overs. However, the introduction of spin turned the game on its head. Chakravarthy (3/14) and Narine (2/26) applied the brakes effectively, triggering a collapse that saw RR slip to 124/5 by the 16th over.
Despite brief resistance from Shimron Hetmyer (15) and Riyan Parag (12), the innings never regained momentum. Kartik Tyagi (3/22) then struck thrice in a crucial phase to derail the lower order, ensuring RR were kept to a below-par total.

Sensing an opportunity, the batters go for a risky second, but Arora recovers quickly, gathers cleanly and fires a sharp throw back to the striker’s end. Seifert is alert this time, whipping off all three stumps in a flash—with Archer nowhere in the frame.
